item.title: Neural Network Quantum States
item.description: In perhaps one of the most well-known treatises in physics — the Principia Mathematica, Isaac Newton tries to explain the motion of the Moon under the gravitational influence of both the Sun and the Earth. At the time, Newton argued that “it would exceed the force of human wit to consider so many causes of motion at the same time”. Physics has come a long way since the times of Newton, but this question, called the many-body problem, troubles us to this day.

item.title: Projects
item.description: Modelling Neutron Star Parameters using Bayesian Neural Networks A Study Oriented Project under Dr Kinjal Banerjee, Department of Physics The Equation of State (EOS) of a neutron star enables us to deduce its structure and behaviour; the EOS is often parameterised by certain key Nuclear Matter Parameters (NMPs) which can be used to form the EOS and subsequently to deduce important properties about the star. In this project, I am attempting to develop a Bayesian neural network-based model to predict neutron star properties such as its mass, radius and tidal deformability using a multivariate Gaussian distribution of the NMPs as the input.

item.title: About
item.description: Hi there! I’m Ameya. I’m a fourth-year undergraduate at BITS, Pilani - K. K. Birla Goa Campus where I’m majoring in Physics and Computer Science. Currently, I’m exploring multiple areas of study at the intersection of physics and computer science, but something I’m really fascinated by is the emerging area of Quantum Machine Learning and neural network representations of quantum states. I’m also interested in quantum computing approaches to ameliorate existing numerical methods.
